💬 Chat with AuthorClaw

Hello! I'm AuthorClaw, your AI writing partner. How can I help you today?

📰 Recent Activity

No recent activity. Create a goal or start a conversation to get going!

🔑 API Key Management

Keys are encrypted with AES-256-GCM and stored in the vault. Values are never displayed.

Can't paste? Put your keys in text files in the VM shared folder, then click Load:

Expected files: gemini_api_key.txt, telegram_bot_token.txt, deepseek_api_key.txt, anthropic_api_key.txt, openai_api_key.txt
Or a single key.txt file will be loaded into the selected key above.

🤖 AI Providers

Loading...

🎯 Model Selection

Override the model name for each active provider. Leave blank to use the default.

Loading providers...

💰 Budget Settings

💓 Heartbeat Settings

Enable Reminders

🤖 Autonomous Mode

When enabled, AuthorClaw wakes up on schedule, checks for active goals, and executes steps automatically. It respects quiet hours and stops when there's nothing to do.

Autonomous Mode OFF
Loading status...

How often AuthorClaw checks for work (default: 30)

Safety limit — how many goal steps per cycle (default: 5)

AuthorClaw won't wake up during quiet hours. Set both to 0 to disable.

📡 Telegram Integration

Loading...

Create a bot with @BotFather on Telegram. Save the token in API Keys above.

Your Telegram user ID. Send /start to @userinfobot to get it.

🌐 Research Domains

Approved domains for AI research. One per line.

🧠 Memory

AuthorClaw remembers conversations and project context across sessions. Reset memory for a clean slate.

💬 Test Connection

📖 Book Project Configuration

Configure your book project here instead of editing code. Saved as JSON to the server.

🎯 Create a Goal

Tell AuthorClaw what you want to achieve. It will autonomously plan the steps, choose the right tools and prompts, and execute them.

AuthorClaw will auto-detect the goal type and create a step-by-step plan.

📋 Goal Templates

Or choose a pre-built goal template:

📰 Director's Log

No activity yet

Run npx tsx scripts/book-conductor.ts in the VM terminal to start.

Sends a stop signal to the conductor. It will finish the current step and shut down gracefully.
